source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The ROC (region of convergence) of the z-transform of a discrete-time signal is represented by the shaded region in the z-plane. If the signal \( x[n] = (2.0)^{|n|} \), \(-\infty < n < +\infty\), then the ROC of its z-transform is represented by
extraction_notes: image field contains an in-memory PIL image placeholder, not a path/URL
raw_answer_text mapped from option letter D to corresponding choice text

image_dependency: Question asks which shaded ROC region in the z-plane matches the signal, so the final selection depends on inspecting diagrams
All choices are diagram-based (A/B/C/D described only as diagrams), not textually specified
multi_step: Solving requires deriving the z-transform ROC for the two-sided signal x[n]=(2.0)^{|n|}
This typically involves splitting the sequence into n>=0 and n<0 parts, finding each partial ROC, and combining/intersecting them
After analytic derivation, the result must be matched to one of four visual diagram options
verifiability: Answer is a single discrete option ('diagram D'), which is easy to compare against the provided choices
The underlying ROC reasoning is mathematically checkable from the explicit signal definition
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true and the image asset exists, so the needed materials for verification are present

decision_reasons: Asset registry passed with no reported issues; question text, answer, and primary image are all present
Initial scores are strong for image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and verifiability
Although the answer is a diagram label and the visual options are not textualized, the required image asset exists, so the core semantics remain recoverable and checkable
This is an image-dependent multiple-choice sample with intact supporting assets, so it is suitable for the candidate pool
